ip_outcome_note: Depicted rigid spiky blue-grey shelled cocoon creature with intense angled eyes matches Pupitar's canonical design exactly. National Pokedex no. 247. Rock/Ground-type. Stage 1 evolution from Larvitar. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-87).

trivia_bullets.text: Pupitar (National Pokedex no. 247) is a Rock/Ground-type Stage 1 Pokemon introduced in Generation II (Johto). It is the middle stage of the Larvitar → Pupitar → Tyranitar pseudo-legendary line. Tyranitar is Johto's pseudo-legendary (base stat total 600), making Pupitar the cocoon before that endpoint. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-87; WebSearch snippet: pokemondb.net Pupitar]

Flavor text: "Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power." — the TCG Rocket Evolution attack directly encodes this narrative: Pupitar can forcibly evolve itself mid-battle by blasting into Tyranitar. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-87]

Larvitar was trivia'd in wave 11 for this archive. The DAA set contains Larvitar (DAA-086) and Pupitar (DAA-087) as sequential card numbers, continuing the line. The Tyranitar endpoint is absent from this set's main body — the line is left open, which the Rocket Evolution attack mechanically resolves if a Tyranitar is in the deck. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-87; wave 11 Larvitar trivia cross-reference]

related_cards.name: Tyranitar
related_cards.note: Final pseudo-legendary evolution (Rock/Dark); Pupitar's Rocket Evolution attack targets this card in the deck

related_cards.name: Larvitar
related_cards.note: Base of pseudo-legendary line; first appeared in Gold & Silver
